Added select_sandbox() [4c175fb] with hard-fail posture: BwrapSandbox (ADR 0009) when bwrap can sandbox, else REFUSE unless PI_ALLOW_UNSANDBOXED=1 (loud-warning fallback to NullSandbox). Wired into all three real-pi acceptance harnesses (phase2/3/6) [e45714b], after the require_pi_and_model gate so no-pi hosts skip cleanly. ADR 0009 accepted [b73fa2b] with trust-boundary framing + isolation ladder. Agents can no longer run unisolated by accident. Deferred validation-step sandboxing tracked as o2t. Gates: 390 passed, ty clean, ruff clean, smoke green.

title: Haskell placeholder data types in Ports.hs provide no structural verification.
description: - **Promotion rationale:** large effort, scope decision needed
notes: Define the record structures for `Package`, `RawTelemetry`, etc., in Haskell to provide actual type safety for the `runTrial` signature. Currently, these are empty declarations that only allow the code to compile without enforcing any invariants.

title: Phase 6.5: Acceptance test — surrogate-directed end-to-end
description: End-to-end acceptance test with real Pi and SurrogateProposer. Seed the optimizer with a biased history (packages that perform poorly on tokens), then verify that the surrogate-directed proposer recommends a package expected to improve on that axis. Mirrors Phase 3 and Phase 4 acceptance test patterns (ADR 0010 fast/full variants).
acceptance_criteria: test_phase6_acceptance_fast and test_phase6_acceptance_full both pass against real Pi with GEMINI_API_KEY. After seeding history with token-heavy packages, the next proposal has a distinct package_hash (surrogate found something different). Final Pareto frontier contains at least one entry. Both markers work: -m acceptance_fast and -m acceptance_full.
close_reason: Phase 6.5 acceptance test green against real Pi: acceptance_fast (1 trial, 4.7s) + acceptance_full (2 trials, 3m11s). Seeds above-bootstrap token-heavy history, asserts surrogate fit + distinct proposals + non-empty frontier. 343 unit tests pass, ty clean.

title: Phase 6.4: SurrogateProposer replaces RandomFromSlotSpace
description: Implement SurrogateProposer as a ProposerPort that composes the GP surrogate (6.2) and EHVI acquisition (6.3): fit surrogate on trial history, maximise EHVI over SlotSpace, return argmax candidate. Below bootstrap threshold delegates to RandomFromSlotSpace. Wire SurrogateProposer as the default proposer in OptimizerDriver.
design: SurrogateProposer(surrogate: SurrogateModelPort, slot_space: SlotSpace, n_bootstrap: int = 10) -> ProposerPort. Acquisition maximisation: evaluate EHVI at all SlotSpace points (feasible for current space sizes; revisit with MC optimiser if space grows >10k).
acceptance_criteria: OptimizerDriver with SurrogateProposer completes a 3-trial run without error. Below N0 trials the proposer returns a valid Package. Above N0 the proposer returns the EHVI argmax (verified via mock surrogate with known optimum). RandomFromSlotSpace remains available as a non-default option.
close_reason: SurrogateProposer composes HetGP+EHVI with random fallback; weighted-objective orientation in acquisition; 4-axis frontier (subjective deferred); driver 3-trial integration test green; 343 tests pass, ty clean

title: Phase 6.3: EHVI acquisition function
description: Implement Expected Hypervolume Improvement (EHVI) as the acquisition function that scores candidate packages against the current Pareto frontier. Active only above the bootstrap threshold (N₀ ≈ 10 trials); below that the caller falls back to random. EHVI computational cost is comfortable at 4D/5D over hundreds of trials — revisit if frontier dimensionality grows.
design: Wrap BoTorch qExpectedHypervolumeImprovement or analytic EHVI. Reference point set to nadir of current frontier minus a margin. Scalarisation over 5 objectives; partitioning via BoTorch NondominatedPartitioning.
acceptance_criteria: EHVI returns higher scores for points expected to dominate frontier members. Returns 0 (or raises) below bootstrap threshold. Agrees with BoTorch reference implementation on small fixtures. Acquisition maximisation selects a different candidate than random baseline on a biased synthetic history.
close_reason: qLogEHVI acquisition over ModelListGP; 9 tests, ty clean, all 331 passing

title: Phase 6.2: Heteroscedastic GP surrogate model
description: Implement a multi-output Heteroscedastic GP surrogate with 5 independent heads — one per Pareto axis: mean_tokens, mean_dollars, scaling_slope, mean_quality, subjective. Each head uses an RBF-ARD kernel (per-dimension length-scales) and input-dependent noise σ²(x) = exp(g(x)) where g ~ GP. Enforce bootstrap discipline: below N₀ ≈ 10 completed trials the surrogate is untrained and the proposer falls back to RandomFromSlotSpace.
design: Use BoTorch / GPyTorch for GP primitives. One SingleTaskGP (or HeteroscedasticSingleTaskGP) per output axis, wrapped behind a SurrogateModelPort. Noise model: inferred GP over log-noise g(x). Hyperparameter optimisation via marginal log-likelihood maximisation (fit_gpytorch_mll). Keep torch optional/lazy-imported so the non-GPU path still passes tests.
acceptance_criteria: GP fits without error on synthetic trial history of length >= N0. Posterior mean and variance are well-defined at query points. Bootstrap guard: predict() raises or returns None when called with fewer than N0 training points. ARD length-scales are learnable hyperparameters. Unit tests cover: single-trial history (bootstrap path), N0+1 trials (surrogate path), heteroscedastic noise structure.
notes: Framework decision captured in ADR 0016 (Proposed): BoTorch/GPyTorch on PyTorch. Start with fixed-observed-noise SingleTaskGP per axis (noise from capability_profile variance), not the unstable HeteroscedasticSingleTaskGP. torch enters lazily behind SurrogateModelPort. Implementation blocked on ADR 0016 acceptance.
close_reason: HetGPSurrogate implemented (BoTorch SingleTaskGP+train_Yvar, 5 heads, bootstrap guard). SurrogateModelPort protocol defined. build_training_data extracts per-axis training data from trial history. 28 new tests green, ty clean.

title: Phase 6.1: Featurize Package into a numeric vector
description: Map Package to a fixed-length float feature vector φ(x) ∈ ℝ^d using the Phase 3.1 slot/value schema. Each slot contributes a one-hot block; the concatenation is the input to all GP heads. This is the prerequisite for all surrogate-model and acquisition work.
design: One-hot encode each slot value; concatenate blocks in a stable, schema-defined order. d = sum of cardinalities across all slots. The encoding must be stable across Python runs (deterministic ordering, not dict-insertion order).
acceptance_criteria: φ is deterministic and injective over the current SlotSpace. Round-trip φ(p1) == φ(p1) and φ(p1) != φ(p2) for distinct packages. Feature dimension d matches the slot schema and is exposed as a constant. Unit tests cover edge cases (single-slot space, all slots populated).
close_reason: FeatureEncoder implemented in domain/featurize.py; 13 unit tests green; ty clean

title: Phase 4/5 reconciliation
description:
Single bead for the documentation, precursor, and plan drift identified during Phase 4 closeout reflection.

Scope:

1. Pre-Phase-4 doc drift (predates Phase 4 but uncovered during reflection):
   - docs/terminology.md L93: '(mean_cost, scaling_slope, mean_quality)' is pre-ADR 0005 framing; should split mean_cost into mean_tokens + mean_dollars.
   - docs/user-journeys.md L90 + L112: same singular mean_cost framing; same fix.
   - docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md L161-171: Metrics code block omits cost_dollars (ADR 0005 added it); surrounding prose still future-tense ('cost_dollars will join').

2. Plan clarifications in docs/implementation-plan.md:
   - Phase 5.4 should mention the 4D -> 5D Pareto lift that subjective scoring drives.
   - Phase 6.2 should say the GP has 4-5 output heads matching the Pareto axes.
   - Phase 6.3 should note EHVI computational cost grows with frontier dimensionality.

3. Haskell precursor reconciliation (at-abstraction-level only per project memory project_haskell_dsl_role.md):
   - Add scalingSlope :: Float to AgentSpace.Metrics.
   - Lift metricsDominate / paretoFrontier to 4D with slope as minimized axis.
   - Update predictPerformance zeroVariance to include slope.
   - Update AgentSpaceSpec.hs tests to 4D; rename '3D' descriptions; add slope-driven dominance test.
   - Strike obsolete 'Phase 4.4 lifts this to 4D once scalingSlope lands' comment block.

4. Close stale bead pi-agent-space-1e0 (cross-language Pareto duplication): its premise ('3D dominance rules are implemented identically in Haskell and Python') is no longer accurate; the Haskell update here reconciles the dimensional drift, after which the rules are again identical (at 4D).

5. Math.pdf paper-level deltas — recorded for user (paper's lead author), NOT edited by this bead:
   - Metric/Pareto-signature object: projections grow from (tokens, dollars, quality) to (tokens, dollars, slope, quality).
   - Dominance morphism: reads slope-on-dollars as the fourth projection; slope-on-tokens stays diagnostic.
   - Below paper level (do NOT need paper update): event-stream schema, profile (mean/variance/p95) decomposition, eval-event difficulty, OLS slope formula, persistence layout.
design:
math.pdf paper-level deltas from Phase 4 (for user — paper's lead author):

Under the category-theory framing where what matters is morphisms between defined categories + the object properties that participate in those morphisms, two paper-level deltas land:

1. Metric / Pareto-signature object: projections grow from (tokens, dollars, quality) to (tokens, dollars, scaling_slope, quality). 'scaling_slope' is a new property of the trial-level metric object.

2. Dominance morphism: reads slope-on-cost-dollars as the fourth projection. Slope-on-tokens stays diagnostic (lives in the metric object but does NOT participate in dominance). The dominance morphism's signature is unchanged (still a partial order on metric tuples), but the projections it consults extend.

Explicitly below paper level (the paper does NOT need to express these):
- Event-stream schema and metric_record event shape (ADR 0012)
- CapabilityProfile decomposition into (mean, variance, p95, n_samples)
- 'difficulty' field on the eval event payload
- The OLS-vs-log-linear choice for slope computation (internal recipe)
- Persistence layout, retry budgets, sandbox details

Reference: implementation lives in python/src/pi_evaluator/domain/pareto.py and python/src/pi_evaluator/domain/capability_profile.py as of ADR 0012 / commits c57a473, daf8c1f, 6fbba68.
acceptance_criteria: All docs in (1) and (2) updated. Haskell tests in haskell/test/AgentSpaceSpec.hs pass with 4D Pareto. pi-agent-space-1e0 closed with reason. Math.pdf items recorded in bead notes for user follow-up. mise run test, lint, typecheck all green.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement ADR 0011: event-stream-first outcome classifier
description: ADR 0011 (Accepted) routes outcome resolution through a single classifier that reads the per-trial event stream first, falling back to the telemetry predicate. Today TrialRunner.run_trial has two disjoint outcome paths: the cost-cap watchdog directly assigns outcome="boundary_violation" at line 164, and _classify_outcome handles everything else via lifecycle.is_model_error over per-problem telemetry. This refactor unifies them.
acceptance_criteria: A new classifier function (likely lifecycle.classify_outcome(events, per_problem_telemetry) or domain/outcome.py — decided at implementation time per ADR 0011's Consequences) returns Outcome based on: (1) any TrialEvent with phase=="boundary_violation" → "boundary_violation"; (2) else lifecycle.is_model_error over any per_problem_telemetry → "error_escalated"; (3) else "completed". TrialRunner.run_trial invokes it exactly once after the per-problem loop and watchdog emissions settle, then writes outcome into the finalized event payload and Trial.outcome. The watchdog override path at trial_runner.py:157-164 (the boundary_tripped → outcome = "boundary_violation" assignment) is removed; the watchdog still emits the boundary_violation event, just stops assigning outcome directly. Existing tests against cost-cap boundary_violation behavior continue to pass with no behavioral change (the event still emits; the classifier reads it). lifecycle.py docstring updates to reflect the new contract — is_model_error is the telemetry predicate the classifier consults; boundary_violation event emission is owned by watchdogs.
notes: No behavioral change in v1: the cost-cap watchdog already emits its boundary_violation event before the override at line 164. This refactor just removes the override and routes through the classifier. Downstream: pi-agent-space-1vc (subprocess-timeout → boundary_violation trigger) becomes mechanical after this lands — the timeout watchdog emits the event with reason: "subprocess_timeout" and the classifier picks it up with no further change. See ADR 0011 Reconsider Triggers for the Phase 4.2 evolution path (Option C: pure event-stream classifier when error_retry / error_escalated event phases land).
close_reason: ADR 0011 classifier landed: lifecycle.classify_outcome(events, per_problem_telemetry) is now the single source for Trial.outcome. TrialRunner.run_trial's watchdog override branch is gone; cost-cap watchdog still emits the boundary_violation event and breaks the loop, but no longer assigns outcome directly. 7 new lifecycle tests cover the classifier directly (precedence, multi-problem, multi-event). 199 tests pass (was 192); ty + ruff clean.

title: Subprocess-timeout → boundary_violation outcome trigger (Phase 3.4 owed; ADR 0007 A2)
description: docs/implementation-plan.md L53 lists this as still-owed by Phase 3.4. When the subprocess timeout fires (separate issue), the trial must be classified outcome="boundary_violation" per ADR 0007 A2's source-of-kill rule — not error_escalated. Currently, even when the timeout mechanism lands, lifecycle.is_model_error's exit_code != 0 rule would misattribute a timeout kill as a model error (the same misattribution that spike 0008 flags for bwrap sandbox-kill).
acceptance_criteria: A subprocess-timeout kill in CliSubprocessAdapter produces a Trial with outcome="boundary_violation" and a finalized event payload reflecting that outcome. lifecycle.is_model_error (or its successor) does NOT misclassify a timeout as a model error. The boundary_violation trigger emits the matching lifecycle event phase per Phase 4.2's design. TDD: a stub harness that simulates TimeoutExpired yields outcome boundary_violation; lifecycle predicates are not exercised on timeout paths.
notes: DEPENDS ON: pi-agent-space-y55 (the timeout mechanism itself) and pi-agent-space-z3w (the ADR 0011 classifier refactor). With ADR 0011 in place, this issue is mechanical: the timeout watchdog emits a TrialEvent with phase="boundary_violation" and payload reason="subprocess_timeout"; the classifier reads it and assigns outcome="boundary_violation" with no further routing logic needed.
close_reason: TrialRunner.run_trial now catches subprocess.TimeoutExpired from self._harness.run() and emits a boundary_violation event with reason='subprocess_timeout', problem_id, and timeout_seconds in the payload. The ADR 0011 classifier reads the event and returns outcome='boundary_violation'. 3 new tests cover single-problem timeout, metric zeroing, and mid-loop timeout (third problem never scheduled). 206 tests pass (was 203); ty + ruff clean.

title: Persistent-error preservation queue (Phase 3.4 owed; ADR 0007 B1)
description: docs/implementation-plan.md L53 lists the persistent-error preservation queue as still-owed by Phase 3.4. ADR 0007 commits to: persistent errors preserve the trial's workspace + telemetry + stderr on disk and queue for asynchronous human classification (no auto-re-propose by the driver). Today, the trial directory IS preserved when retry_budget exhausts (the materialized workspace lives on disk), but there is no explicit queue or surface for human review.
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated mechanism surfaces error_escalated trials for asynchronous human classification — minimally, a discoverable list (file, jsonl, or driver-emitted index) of preserved error trials. Existing trial-directory preservation behavior (per ADR 0007 B1) is preserved; this work adds the queue/index, not the preservation itself. TDD: a driver run with N retries exhausted produces a queue entry pointing at the trial directory; the queue is durable across process restarts.
notes: DESIGN PINNED in docs/design-notes.md (commit da12ae4): the queue is a derived view over PersistencePort.load_trials() filtered on outcome=='error_escalated'. NOT a separate file written at finalize time. Implementation surface: a one-line helper (free function or PerTrialDirectoryAdapter staticmethod) + 2-3 tests confirming filter correctness. Phase 3.4 closure (commits 2a56a2e and 6c82379) covered the circuit-breaker and adapter-layer retry budget; this issue completes ADR 0007 B1's queue commitment. If spike 0009 (driver-run event log) lands first, the scanner naturally moves to scan the driver event log instead — same discipline, different source.
close_reason: Closed

title: CliSubprocessAdapter: subprocess timeout (Phase 3.4 owed; ADR 0007 A2)
description: docs/implementation-plan.md L53 lists subprocess timeout as still-owed by Phase 3.4. Today subprocess.run is called without a timeout, so a wedged Pi invocation will block the driver indefinitely. ADR 0007 A2 commits to timeouts being source-of-kill-classified as boundary violations.
acceptance_criteria: CliSubprocessAdapter.run accepts a per-trial timeout (configurable, with a non-disruptive default). Timeout enforcement uses subprocess.run(..., timeout=...) and surfaces TimeoutExpired distinctly from other exit-nonzero cases. Unit tests cover both 'timeout fires' and 'completion under budget' paths without real time waits (via injectable sleep / mocked subprocess).
notes: Pairs with the boundary_violation trigger work (filed separately); this issue lands the timeout itself, the trigger wires it to outcome classification. ADR 0007 A2 reference: timeouts → boundary_violation. Spike 0008's resolution may affect HOW the outcome flows (watchdog vs. event-stream classifier), but the underlying timeout mechanism is independent of that decision.
close_reason: Adapter takes subprocess_timeout_seconds: float | None = None; subprocess.run() now receives timeout=. subprocess.TimeoutExpired propagates past the retry loop unhandled per ADR 0007 A2 (timeouts are boundary violations, not retryable model errors). 4 new adapter tests cover default-no-enforcement, completion-under-budget, timeout-fires-raises, and timeout-bypasses-retries. 203 tests pass (was 199); ty + ruff clean. The TrialRunner catch + boundary_violation emission is tracked under pi-agent-space-1vc, now unblocked.

title: There is no CHANGELOG.md or equivalent to track user-visible changes across releases. While Phase 3 closeout is mentioned in README.md, there is no structured history for consumers to audit features, fixes, or breaking changes.
notes: Create a root CHANGELOG.md following the 'Keep a Changelog' format and back-populate it from recent git tags and the implementation plan phases.

title: The project does not declare a versioning scheme in README.md or docs/. Consumers cannot distinguish whether MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH increments follow Semantic Versioning, Calendar Versioning, or a custom stability contract.
notes: Add a 'Versioning' section to the root README.md declaring the project's adherence to Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 (or ZeroVer if stability is not yet promised).

title: CliSubprocessAdapter.run retries the subprocess execution against the *same materialized workspace*. Since the agent run may have mutated the workspace before failing, the retry will run in a corrupted/dirty state.
notes: Move `materialize_workspace(workspace)` inside the retry loop so each attempt runs against a pristine copy.
close_reason: Superseded by accepted decision ADR 0007 B1. The 'same materialized workspace' retry behavior is deliberate, not a defect: B1 explicitly preserves any state Pi started building before a transient error, and lists 'retries against a possibly-mutated workspace could compound errors' as a known, accepted Con. The prescribed fix (materialize inside the retry loop for a pristine copy per attempt) is exactly the rejected alternative B2. Reversing it would require amending ADR 0007 B1, inverting test_retries_use_same_materialized_workspace, and rewriting the cli_subprocess_adapter docstring. If the dirty-workspace risk is later judged to outweigh state-preservation, reopen via a spike/ADR amendment (B1 vs B2) rather than as a P1 bug.

title: CliSubprocessAdapter implements a retry backoff schedule `(30.0, 60.0)` without jitter. This risks thundering-herd synchronization if multiple evaluators encounter transient upstream errors simultaneously.
notes: Add jitter to the backoff duration in the retry loop (e.g., `sleep_time = backoff * random.uniform(0.5, 1.5)`).
close_reason: Added multiplicative jitter (RETRY_JITTER_RANGE=(0.5,1.5)) to each adapter-layer retry backoff in CliSubprocessAdapter, via an injectable random_uniform callable (mirrors the existing sleep DI). Prevents thundering-herd lockstep across concurrent evaluators hitting the same transient upstream error. Tests: test_backoff_applies_jitter, test_default_jitter_stays_within_range; existing test_backoff_schedule_passed_to_sleep pins jitter to 1.0. Docstring + design-notes.md updated.

Docs-only; verified the model strings and selection logic against acceptance_support.py and that .env.example stays tracked while .env is ignored.

title: Lack of a central typed configuration registry. Operational parameters (cost caps, retry budgets, circuit breakers) are scattered across class constructors and hardcoded defaults rather than being consolidated into a single, typed source of truth.
notes: Introduce a central Settings class (e.g., using Pydantic Settings) to define, type, and document all configuration variables in one place.
close_reason: Central typed Settings registry delivered: pi_evaluator/config.py (frozen dataclass + from_env), edge-layer, hexagonal injection preserved. ADR 0023. Composition-root wiring tracked in 2gf. Commit 44c6e5a.

title: No automated CI gating path (e.g., GitHub Actions) is configured to enforce test, lint, and type-check invariants on pull requests.
notes: Workflow delivered: .github/workflows/ci.yml — Python gate (lint, typecheck, test) on PRs + push to main, mise (scoped python/uv) + uv --frozen from the ADR 0021 locks, SHA-pinned actions, actionlint-clean. Committed 156f1b4 (local). REMAINING: (1) push so Actions runs for the first time; (2) branch protection requiring the 'Python gate' check on main — a repo-admin setting, needs operator decision (gh api or UI). Haskell job deferred → 73x (blocked on 8wr).
close_reason: CI gate delivered: .github/workflows/ci.yml (Python lint+typecheck+test), pushed, first run green (27084871844). Branch protection on main requires the 'Python gate' check (strict=false, enforce_admins=false to preserve direct-push flow). Python-scoped per ADR 0021; Haskell job tracked in 73x (blocked on 8wr).

title: Lifecycle classification logic (ADR 0007) is duplicated between `TrialRunner._has_model_error` and `CliSubprocessAdapter._is_retryable_error`.
description:
The code comments explicitly acknowledge this duplication to avoid an orchestrator-to-adapter dependency, but it creates a correctness risk where the two predicates can drift.
Both the runner and the adapter should import from this stable domain module.
notes: Extract the shared model-error predicate into a dedicated domain module, e.g., `pi_evaluator/domain/lifecycle.py`, as planned in `implementation-plan.md` L144.
close_reason: Resolved in commit 2611d45 (refactor 3.5.1): python/src/pi_evaluator/lifecycle.py extracts the model-error predicate; _has_model_error and _is_retryable_error no longer exist in src.

title: Optimizer composition-root CLI (pi-eval optimize)
description: There is no production composition root that constructs OptimizerDriver — it is built only in tests. Build a 'pi-eval optimize' subcommand (mirroring pi-eval score: argparse, direct adapter wiring, no port, PEP 420) that is the application startup: it reads Settings.from_env(), calls Settings.validate() (the gfm startup-validation call site, retiring its dead-code-in-src status), then assembles CliSubprocessAdapter -> TrialRunner -> proposer + PerTrialDirectoryAdapter -> OptimizerDriver and runs driver.run(trial_budget). Also front it with a 'mise run optimize' task (ADR 0005 refers to caps as 'per mise run optimize invocation'). See ADR 0023 'Future optimize CLI surface' notes for the full scoping: trial_budget/eval_suite_ref/slot-space are per-invocation flags (not Settings fields); config precedence (flag>env>default) is unresolved and must be decided; provider/model selection should reuse the .env.example 'first key set' precedence so validate() and model choice never disagree; re-evaluate argparse-vs-Click as the second subcommand.
notes: Blocked on ADR 0023 config registry landing (Settings + validate). Captures the gfm startup hook that 0023 deliberately deferred.

title: S007: observability suite — operational metrics + phase tracing (ADR 0022)
description: Extend ADR 0015 (logging, done) with the other two observability pillars: operational run metrics (outcome counts, cost, wallclock, trials/min) persisted as run_summary.json, and per-phase tracing (span durations). Thin ObservabilityPort seam with an in-process default adapter (stdlib-only); OTel deferred behind the enterprise trigger. Closes spike S007.
close_reason: S007 settled as ADR 0022 (Accepted) and implemented in three slices: (1) ObservabilityPort + InProcessObservability/NullObservability adapters + RunSummary/SpanStats domain types + shared run_paths.run_dir [12 tests]; (2) wired phase spans into TrialRunner and run-level counters/trial-span/finish_run into OptimizerDriver, Null default leaves the existing path unchanged [2 e2e tests]; (3) ADR 0022 + implementation-plan (S007 closed) + threat-model A5 doc-sync. Gates green: 434 passed, ty clean, ruff clean, smoke PASS.

title: OS-level resource caps: systemd-run cgroup wrap around bwrap (ADR 0020 D2, ADR 0009 rung 1+)
description: The orchestration cost (ADR 0005) and wallclock (ADR 0007) caps bound the common runaway but not CPU/memory/fd/fork-bomb between cap checks. Wrap the bwrap invocation in 'systemd-run --scope' with MemoryMax/CPUQuota/TasksMax so the OS enforces hard caps. Realizes rung 1+ of the ADR 0009 isolation ladder. Degrade gracefully where systemd-run is absent (run without the cgroup scope, log that caps are unenforced). Cap values operator-configurable with conservative defaults.
notes: v1 commitment per ADR 0020 D2 / threat-model.md A4. Touches the sandbox adapter (BwrapSandbox or select_sandbox seam). Run smoke after.
close_reason: ResourceCappedSandbox decorator wraps BwrapSandbox in systemd-run --scope (MemoryMax/CPUQuota/TasksMax); composed in select_sandbox; degrades gracefully when systemd-run unavailable. 11 new tests green; smoke PASS. ADR 0020 D2.

title: Implement typed event-payload model (ADR 0017, per-phase dataclasses)
description: Per ADR 0017 (Accepted, Option 1): add domain/event_payloads.py with a frozen dataclass per emitted phase (Configured, EvalRecord, MetricRecord, CostCapWarning, BoundaryViolation [reason-discriminated, optional shape fields], Finalized), a sealed EventPayload union, and parse(event)->EventPayload|None dispatching on event.phase via the ADR 0019 _tolerant constructor. Producers in trial_runner construct the dataclass and asdict it (~7 emit sites; on-disk JSON unchanged). capability_profile consumes parse() results, dropping its .get()+None-skip ladders. Event.payload stays dict on Event and on disk. This is the remaining (Event.payload) half of pi-agent-space-3kz. Mirrors the telemetry.py AssistantMessage pattern used for the RawTelemetry half.
design: boundary_violation's timeout vs cost-cap shapes collapse into one dataclass discriminated by reason, shape-specific fields optional. parse reuses _tolerant (963) so unknown fields drop-and-log (D4) and absent additive fields default (D3) — no second parsing mechanism.
close_reason: Typed per-phase event payloads implemented (commit 64c7f5c): event_payloads.py with 6 frozen dataclasses + EventPayload union + parse()/to_payload(), reusing the tolerant seam. All 7 trial_runner emit sites build typed payloads; capability_profile consumes via parse()+isinstance. On-disk JSON unchanged. 372 tests green, ty+ruff clean, smoke passes.

title: Tolerant read seam for persisted JSON (must-ignore + info log, ADR 0019 D4/D8)
description: Per ADR 0019 D4/D8: replace every brittle Model(**json) splat in PerTrialDirectoryAdapter.load_trials and the event-line readers with a tolerant constructor that drops unknown keys (instead of raising TypeError) and logs each ignored key at info ('file newer than reader'), wiring into ADR 0015 structured logging. Applies uniformly across config.json/versions.json/events.jsonl/final.json. This is the must-ignore mechanism that makes forward-compat (older reads newer) safe and is a general read-boundary discipline independent of the version number an individual file carries.
close_reason: domain/tolerant_read.tolerant() implemented (must-ignore + info log, D4/D8); all 6 load_trials splats replaced (commit b1e1a0c). 363 tests green, ty+ruff clean, smoke passes.

title: Add schema_version stamp to persisted trial/run layouts (ADR 0019 D1/D2)
description: Per ADR 0019: stamp a schema_version string (MAJOR.MINOR, mirroring release SemVer at write time) into config.json, governing the whole 4-file trial directory; stamp the run-event log header the same way. Add a version-check on load that classifies same-major (proceed), older (proceed per D3), newer (proceed lossy + log per D4). Patch never changes schema (D2). Implements the write+read half of the policy; depends on nothing but pairs with the tolerant-reader seam.
design: Stamp lives in config.json (required, read-first in load_trials) so no new file is added. Value omits patch. 0.x stamps its real MAJOR.MINOR (e.g. 0.1) per D7.
close_reason: schema_version stamp + non-raising load-time version check implemented in PerTrialDirectoryAdapter (commit 39c87ae), with a drift-guard test pinning SCHEMA_VERSION to pyproject MAJOR.MINOR. 357 tests green, ty+ruff clean, smoke passes.

title: Audit Python↔Haskell precursor drift after Phases 4-6
description: Python (python/src/pi_evaluator/) is the canonical source of truth; the Haskell DSL (haskell/) and docs/math.pdf are precursors that must stay consistent AT THEIR OWN COARSER ABSTRACTION LEVEL — not detail-for-detail (per CLAUDE.local.md and the 'precursors as entry points' design intent). Phases 4-6 added substantial Python concepts (capability profiles + scaling slope, subjective/partial scoring, 5D Pareto, surrogate model + EHVI acquisition + surrogate-directed proposer, typed event/telemetry boundaries) that the Haskell precursor predates. ACTION: review haskell/ against the current Python domain/ports model and flag conceptual drift — abstractions present in Python but absent/contradicted in the DSL, or DSL constructs no longer reflecting the canonical design. Produce a drift list; decide per item whether to update the precursor or record the divergence as intentional. Scope to abstraction-level coherence, not API parity.

D1 float64 mandate (gp_numerics.f64) and D2 explicit Cholesky-jitter backstop (gp_numerics.cholesky_safe) implemented and wired through HetGPSurrogate + EHVIAcquisition. title: Resolve ADR 0015 must-decide items and implement production-grade logging
description: Six architectural choices in ADR 0015 (docs/adrs/0015-structured-logging-depth.md) need decisions before the logging layer is production-grade: MD1 framework, MD2 transport, MD3 context correlation, MD4 naming, MD5 level policy, MD6 PII scrubbing. Once decided, implement the eight must-do commitments: contextvars context binding, QueueHandler isolation, logger.exception() convention, third-party logger audit, and the rest. Likely directions are noted in the ADR Decision section.

Extend domain/pareto.py _pareto_axes() to return None for the subjective axis when score is absent, mirroring the Phase 4 pattern for error_escalated trials.

title: Add structured logging to the project
description:
The project currently uses Python's standard logging module with unstructured string messages (optimizer_driver.py logger.warning calls). Structured logging (e.g. structlog or python-json-logger) would give every log entry consistent machine-readable fields (timestamp, level, logger, plus key-value pairs), making run-level observability queryable without a new filesystem artifact.

Connection to ADR 0013 (S001): structured logging with a file handler writing JSON-lines into the experiment base directory would substantially upgrade option R3 (Python logging only) — it gains durability and queryability at zero PersistencePort cost. It also makes R7 (run_id as correlation key in configured events) immediately useful, since run_id would appear as a structured field on every log line.

Scope: add structured logging to optimizer_driver.py, trial_runner.py, and the harness adapter as a minimum. Choose library (structlog vs python-json-logger vs stdlib JSON formatter). Document the log handler configuration pattern (how operators point the file handler at the experiment directory). Update ADR 0013 Options Considered to reflect the upgraded R3 option once this lands.
close_reason: Closed

Aggregate per-(problem, metric) events into a CapabilityProfile carrying mean / variance / p95 / n_samples / scaling_slope per axis.

Domain placement: new dataclass CapabilityProfile in domain/types.py; accessor on Trial or a free function in domain/capability_profile.py per ADR 0012.
acceptance_criteria: CapabilityProfile domain type + tests landed. Tests cover: trivial single-problem trial (slope=0), monotonic-degradation trial (slope>0 on cost), quality-improving trial. Aggregation re-derivable from events.jsonl alone.

title: Trial event logs (events.jsonl) persist raw telemetry including stderr and malformed lines which may contain sensitive data or API keys without redaction.
notes: v1 commitment per ADR 0020 D1 / threat-model.md A2+A5: redaction layer in the persistence write path scrubs provider-key shapes (sk-…, AIza…, Bearer/x-api-key headers) before lines hit events.jsonl. Defence-in-depth atop MD6-A call-site discipline.
close_reason: Implemented domain/redaction.py applied in PerTrialDirectoryAdapter._append_jsonl; 15 unit + 1 integration test green. ADR 0020 D1.

title: The cost cap warning in `optimizer_driver.py` uses unstructured string interpolation (`logger.warning("Per-run cost cap warning: cumulative=$%.4f...")`) rather than structured event fields, making it difficult to query or aggregate.
notes: Convert the warning to a structured log line (e.g., `logger.warning("per_run_cost_cap_warning", cumulative_cost=cumulative, cap_usd=self._per_run_cost_cap_usd)`).
close_reason: Resolved by Phase 5 structured logging MVP (commit d095c1e). optimizer_driver.py warning is now structured with explicit event fields.

title: Representation of Trial lifecycle allows illegal states (Optional Metrics/Outcome).
notes: Refactor the `Trial` class to use a sum type (Union of dataclasses) representing its lifecycle states (e.g., `ConfiguredTrial`, `EvaluatedTrial`, `FinalizedTrial`). This mirrors the Haskell `Outcome` model and ensures that `final_metrics` and `outcome` are only accessible when the trial is in the appropriate state, eliminating `None` checks.

title: Primitive obsession for domain identifiers and quantities (trial_id, model, tokens, dollars).
notes: Wrap domain primitives in refined types using `NewType` in Python (e.g., `TrialId = NewType('TrialId', str)`) and `newtype` in Haskell (e.g., `newtype Prompt = Prompt String`). This prevents silent argument-swapping and documents intent in function signatures like `finalize_trial(trial_id, metrics, outcome)`.

title: Haskell modeling DSL has relatively low test coverage (19 examples) compared to the core Python implementation.
notes: Expand the Haskell test suite in 'haskell/test/AgentSpaceSpec.hs' to cover more edge cases in the Pareto and Bayesian optimization stubs.

title: The release process is undocumented and lacks a defined gate. There is no guidance on how a commit on main becomes a released version, or what CI/manual checks must pass before tagging.
notes: Document the release flow in CLAUDE.md or a new RELEASE.md, defining the branch strategy (e.g., trunk-based with tags), the version-bump location, and the mandatory quality gates (tests, lints) that must pass before a release is cut.

title: Manifest versions have drifted in formatting: python/pyproject.toml uses '0.1.0' while haskell/pi-agent-space.cabal uses '0.1.0.0'. There is no automated mechanism to keep these in sync during a version bump.
notes: Standardize version formats across all manifest files and implement a 'mise' task (e.g., 'mise run bump') that updates all versions in lockstep using a single source of truth.

title: Lack of automated documentation generation and drift checks. The project has rich docstrings and ADRs but no pipeline to render them into a searchable documentation site or verify in CI that the documentation remains in sync with the code.
notes: Set up a documentation generator (e.g., MkDocs with mkdocstrings) and add a CI job to verify that documentation builds without errors and stays current with the source.

title: Missing declared license posture and audit gate. The project lacks a 'LICENSES.md' or equivalent document, and there is no CI gate ensuring that newly added dependencies comply with an allowlist (Principle 8).
notes: Document the project's license policy in 'LICENSES.md' and add a license audit step (e.g., 'pip-licenses' or 'cargo-deny' equivalent) to the CI pipeline.

title: The project demonstrates high discipline in capturing scope changes and architectural shifts via ADRs (e.g., ADR 0005 for cost axes, ADR 0007 for lifecycle). This ensures that every expansion of the project's scope is documented with rationale and consequences, preventing "smuggled" scope creep.
notes: Continue this practice and ensure that future phase-driven scope expansions (like Phase 6's surrogate modeling) continue to be preceded by an ADR or a design-notes update.
close_reason: Positive observation, not an actionable issue. No work item.

title: The project lacks an explicit "Non-goals" section in the root README or a dedicated purpose document. While "deferred" items are listed in the implementation plan, there is no statement of what the project deliberately refuses to do (e.g., "We do not aim to be a general-purpose agent framework," or "We do not support non-Pi harnesses in v1").
notes: Add a "Non-goals" subsection to the root README.md or a new PURPOSE.md. Enumerate at least three explicit non-goals to prevent future scope creep and align contributor expectations (e.g., no live-traffic production orchestration in v1, no support for non-hexagonal adapters, no built-in GUI).

title: GraduatedProblemSetAdapter and PerTrialDirectoryAdapter call `json.loads` on files without catching `JSONDecodeError`. A malformed file will crash the entire load process.
notes: Wrap `json.loads` in a try/except block to catch `JSONDecodeError`, and either skip the corrupted entry with a warning or propagate a typed domain error.
close_reason: Both load paths (GraduatedProblemSetAdapter.load, PerTrialDirectoryAdapter.load_trials) wrapped json.loads to catch JSONDecodeError and skip the offending entry with a structured warning (events problem_json_malformed / trial_json_malformed) instead of aborting the whole load. Chose skip+warn over typed-error to match the project's resilience posture (tolerant reads, schema-version tolerance). The catch is JSONDecodeError-only, so the existing safety net is intact: a parseable-but-schema-incomplete included problem still raises KeyError (test_no_filter_still_validates_every_problem), and excluded problems still skip pre-validation (test_problem_ids_filter_skips_malformed_excluded_problem). Also fixed the graduated adapter's docstring promise (malformed-but-excluded must not abort) which the pre-filter parse violated for unparseable JSON. Extracted _load_one_trial to scope the per-dir guard. TDD: 2 new caplog tests. Gates: ruff+ty clean, 438 passed, coverage 98.60%, smoke PASS.

title: Deployment credentials (API keys) are likely handled as long-lived environment variables without automated rotation or OIDC scoping.
notes: Transition to short-lived, scoped credentials using OIDC federation (e.g., GitHub Actions OIDC to cloud providers) or a secret management service with automated rotation.

title: No automated or documented rollback mechanism exists for the evaluator system.
notes: Create a scripted 'one-step' rollback procedure (e.g., a `mise run rollback` command) that reverts the system to the previously known-good version and exercises it in a game-day drill.

title: Floating tool versions in 'mise.toml'. Multiple tools (claude, pandoc, weasyprint, beads) and development tools (ruff, ty, uv) are referenced as 'latest' rather than pinned to an immutable version or content digest, breaking build reproducibility (Principle 3).
notes: Partial (ADR 0021 D3): the CI toolchain (python/uv/ruff/ty) is now pinned with per-platform checksums in python/mise.lock, and python/mise.toml no longer uses 'latest'. Remaining: root mise.toml doc/agent tools (claude, gemini, pandoc, weasyprint, beads, committed, git-cliff) still 'latest' — pin + root mise.lock.

title: The project lacks a 'build once, promote many' artifact pipeline. It is currently run directly from source, which risks environment drift.
notes: Define a canonical build artifact (e.g., a Docker image or Python wheel) produced by CI that is used for all downstream evaluation and deployment stages without being rebuilt.

title: DORA metrics (Lead Time, Deployment Frequency, Change Failure Rate, MTTR) are not measured or visible to the team.
notes: Establish a baseline for DORA metrics by tracking deployment events (e.g., successful `mise run build` or tagged releases) and MTTR via issue resolution times. Surface these in a shared dashboard or weekly report.

title: Lack of full environment containerization (no Dockerfile or devcontainer).
notes: Implement a Dockerfile and devcontainer configuration to provide a bit-identical development and execution substrate, as recommended by SLP Principle 4.
close_reason: ADR 0021: Python-only CI/dev container image (Podman, OCI-standard). mise.lock toolchain + uv.lock deps (CPU torch). Verified podman build+run green (418 passed, 2 bwrap skips). Trials stay on host per ADR 0009/0020.

title: The 'pi' agent binary, central to the project's execution, is not pinned in mise.toml or any declarative manifest.
notes: Pin the 'pi' binary version in mise.toml (e.g., using an npm or generic-binary provider) to eliminate tribal knowledge during onboarding and ensure version parity.

title: The root 'mise run setup' command is incomplete: it only triggers Python setup. The Haskell project lacks a setup task and is omitted from the root onboarding path, creating friction for contributors needing the full stack.
notes: Add a 'setup' task to haskell/mise.toml and update the root mise.toml 'setup' task to depend on both setup-python and setup-haskell.

title: Configuration drift risk for retry_budget: the parameter is declared in OptimizerDriver but not passed to the adapter that actually enforces it.
notes: Pass the retry_budget from the OptimizerDriver through the orchestrator to the CliSubprocessAdapter.
close_reason: Deferred to v2 by docs/implementation-plan.md L245 ('What's deferred'): retry_budget is declarative-only in v1; documented in commit cfa9f53 and design-notes.md.

title: Duplicated event stream parsing: logic to filter for assistant 'message_end' events is repeated in synthetic_suite_scorer.py and trial_runner.py.
notes: Provide a shared helper in domain/types.py to extract specific event types from a Pi telemetry stream.
close_reason: Already resolved by prior refactors b2c7ffb (centralize assistant message_end parsing) and 32fa697 (typed AssistantMessage). The shared helper exists as domain/telemetry.py::assistant_messages — a dedicated typed-projection module, cleaner than the issue's suggested domain/types.py. Both former dup sites now use it: adapters/synthetic_suite_scorer.py (line 35) and lifecycle.py (line 46, which is what trial_runner delegates to via classify_outcome). Verified no raw message_end/type filtering remains in trial_runner.py or elsewhere outside telemetry.py. No code change needed.

title: Manual dataclass reconstruction in PerTrialDirectoryAdapter.load_trials duplicates knowledge of field names and types for 5 domain objects.
notes: Use a serialization library or implement from_dict class methods on the dataclasses to handle dictionary-to-object mapping.
close_reason: Already resolved by commit b1e1a0c (ADR 0019 tolerant-read seam, closed pi-agent-space-963). That commit replaced all six Model(**json) reconstructions in load_trials (Package, EvalSuiteRef, VersionVector, TrialEvent, Metrics, SubjectiveScore) with the generic domain/tolerant_read.tolerant(cls, data) — a dataclasses.fields-introspecting constructor that centralizes field-name/type knowledge in one place and additionally tolerates newer-minor-schema files (drops unknown keys). This is the issue's 'serialization library / from_dict' ask, applied uniformly at the domain boundary rather than scattered per-dataclass. All 6 leaf types are flat (no nested dataclasses), so the shallow construction is correct. The remaining manual Trial(...) assembly maps the multi-file on-disk layout (inverse of save_trial), not dataclass fields, so there is no field-knowledge duplication left to remove. Verified gates green on the tolerant seam already. No code change needed.

title: Duplicate Pareto frontier logic (cross-language): 3D dominance rules are implemented identically in Haskell (AgentSpace.hs) and Python (pareto.py).
notes: Designate one implementation as canonical or use a single source of truth to generate or verify both implementations.
close_reason: Superseded by pi-agent-space-98m (Phase 4/5 reconciliation). The original premise — '3D dominance rules are implemented identically in Haskell and Python' — drifted when Phase 4.4 lifted Python to 4D; commit 4c5b17e reconciles Haskell to 4D, restoring identity at the new dimensionality. Cross-language duplication remains by design (the Haskell DSL is an audience-specific entry point, not source-of-truth) and is documented in memory project_haskell_dsl_role.md / docs/architecture/haskell.md.

title: `OptimizerDriver.run` recomputes the entire Pareto frontier (O(N^2)) for all trials in every iteration of its main loop. As the trial budget (N) grows, this quadratic recomputation will dominate execution time.
notes: Implement an incremental Pareto frontier update algorithm or defer frontier recomputation to the end of the run, saving intermediate frontier IDs only if they are needed for real-time reporting.
close_reason: Optimized Pareto frontier computation in OptimizerDriver.run by implementing an incremental update algorithm (add_to_frontier), reducing complexity from O(N^2) per iteration to O(F) where F is the frontier size.

title: Absence of startup configuration validation. The system does not verify that required environment variables (like API keys) or configuration values are present and valid before beginning execution.
notes: Implement a validation step at application startup that checks the configuration registry and aborts with a clear error if required values are missing or malformed.
close_reason: Settings.validate() implemented + fully tested (>=1 provider key, coherent ranges), raising ConfigError. ADR 0023. The live startup call site (Settings.from_env().validate()) is the optimizer composition root, tracked in 2gf. Commit 44c6e5a.

title: Hardcoded 'magic numbers' in production logic: COST_CAP_WARNING_FRACTION and DEFAULT_RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS are defined as module-level constants.
notes: Lift these constants into the central configuration registry so they can be tuned per-environment without code changes.
close_reason: Lifted COST_CAP_WARNING_FRACTION, DEFAULT_RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS, RETRY_JITTER_RANGE into Settings (ADR 0023) as env-tunable defaulted ctor params. Commit e2a122a.

title: ADR back-references in code use numeric IDs (e.g., "ADR 0005") instead of the exact filename slugs (e.g., "0005-trial-cost-and-budget").
description: The `architecture` skill mandates using the exact slug to ensure references are mechanically greppable across the repo and resilient to drift.
notes: Update ADR back-references in docstrings and comments across the Python and Haskell source files to use the full filename slug of the corresponding ADR.

close_reason: Slot/Value space schema landed in Phase 3.1 — python/src/pi_evaluator/domain/slot_space.py. Tag phase-3-complete on origin.

title: Phase 3
close_reason: Phase 3 epic complete; tag phase-3-complete on origin.

title: Add Haskell job to CI workflow
description: The CI gate (51d / .github/workflows/ci.yml) is Python-only for v1: the Haskell toolchain is not fully repo-pinned (only hx; GHC/cabal come from outside) and Haskell has an open P0 (8wr). Add a Haskell job (cabal build + cabal test) once the toolchain is pinned and 8wr is resolved.

title: Validation steps run unsandboxed over agent-authored workspace content (ADR 0009 deferral)
description: ADR 0009 sandboxes the pi invocation but explicitly leaves graduated-problem validation steps OUTSIDE the sandbox (they run as the evaluator user, after the trial, reading workspace contents the agent may have written). The risk surface is validation tooling executing agent-authored content — e.g. pytest importing a conftest.py or test file the agent created. Real but secondary; isolating validation introduces its own complications (shell semantics across --bind views, validation needing network or non-workspace paths). Decide whether/how to sandbox validation: separate bwrap invocation, container mount layers, or accept the risk with a documented rationale.
notes: Surfaced by closing j8x (select_sandbox wiring). See ADR 0009 Scope bullet 'Validation steps are not sandboxed in v1' and the 'Validation-step taint' reconsider trigger.

title: MD6-B: field-allowlist redaction in JsonFormatter (enterprise-gated)
description: Deferred per ADR 0015 MD6 decision. v1 uses MD6-A (call-site discipline). When the enterprise deployment scenario becomes load-bearing (logs leave the local R&D machine), implement MD6-B: JsonFormatter emits only pre-approved field names; unknown fields dropped or replaced with <redacted>. Track together with pi-agent-space-28g (events.jsonl raw-stderr redaction) so the redaction question lives in one place. Trigger: enterprise deployment scenario (three-scenario model, scenario 2).
notes: Cross-link: pi-agent-space-28g (events.jsonl raw stderr/malformed-line redaction). Both are the deferred redaction surface; resolve together at enterprise scenario.

title: Dependency-deprecation watch: torch.jit.script in GPyTorch/linear_operator
description: GPyTorch/linear_operator still use torch.jit.script, which newer torch flags as deprecated (use torch.compile/torch.export). Not our code; emitted transitively when the GP machinery loads. Currently silenced by a targeted pytest filterwarnings ignore in python/pyproject.toml ('ignore:`torch.jit.script` is deprecated:DeprecationWarning'). ACTION: on any torch/gpytorch/botorch bump, re-check whether the warning is gone upstream and remove the filter if so (don't let it mask a real future torch.jit removal). Pinned at time of filing: torch=2.12.0 gpytorch=1.15.2 botorch=0.17.2 linear_operator=0.6.1. Context: ADR 0016 (torch stack).

title: Fix pre-existing E501 lint debt in test/adapter files
description: mise run lint (ruff check .) reports 19 pre-existing E501 line-too-long errors on main, unrelated to Phase 6 work: tests/test_persistence.py (9), tests/test_cli_score.py (7), tests/test_logging_config.py (1), tests/test_surrogate_data.py (1), src/pi_evaluator/adapters/per_trial_directory_adapter.py (1). Running 'mise run format' (ruff check --fix + ruff format) repo-wide resolves them but reformats many files, so it should be done as a dedicated commit. The repo lint gate is effectively non-blocking today; this cleans it up.
notes: Commit 5e6e4e8 (2026-06-01) made adjacent lint/type hygiene fixes folded under this issue: annotated FastNondominatedPartitioning local as Any in test_ehvi_acquisition.py (clears ty false positive -> mise run typecheck fully green) and ruff --fix import-sort (I001) in test_logging_config.py + test_surrogate_data.py. The 17 E501 line-length errors this issue tracks remain OPEN.
close_reason: All 17 E501 line-length errors wrapped by hand in commit f0edb83; `ruff check` now reports zero errors across the tree.
